response: The global food system faces mounting strain as the population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring 60% more food production. This challenge is intensified by climate change, water scarcity, and soil degradation, while agriculture itself generates about 25% of greenhouse gas emissions and 33% of the world’s arable land has already lost productivity. The text argues that food waste, which affects roughly one-third of all food produced, further worsens the crisis. It highlights potential solutions such as AI-driven precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crops, regenerative farming, and policy reforms, but stresses that scaling these affordably for the poorest populations remains the central challenge.
